title: Telling time word problems (within the hour)
description: Solve a word problem to find the duration of an event.  Both analog or digital clocks are included.
topic_path: Module 2: Place value and problem solving with units of measure
Topic A: Time measurement and problem solving
Telling time word problems (within the hour)

assessment_items.question_content:
Mateo started riding his bike to a friend's house at $4{:}08 \text { p.m.}$ and finished riding at $4{:}22  \text { p.m.}$

**How long did Mateo ride?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] minutes
hints:
To find out how long Mateo rode, we can find the distance from $\blueD{4{:}08}$ to $\blueD{4{:}22}$.


[[☃ image 1]]
$\purpleD2\text{ minutes} + \goldE{10}\text{ minutes} + \greenD{2}\text{ minutes}  = 14\text{ minutes}$
Mateo rode for $14$ minutes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Joey started making a clay dragon at  $8{:}05 \text { p.m.}$ and finished at $8{:}57 \text { p.m.}$
   

**How long did Joey  spend making a clay dragon?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] minutes
hints:
To find out how long Joey spent making a clay dragon, we can find the distance from $\blueD{8{:}05}$ to $\blueD{8{:}57}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\goldE{50}\text{ minutes} + \greenD{2}\text{ minutes}  = 52\text{ minutes}$
Joey spent $52$ minutes making a clay dragon.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ella started making a birthday card for a friend at $7{:}19\text { p.m.}$ and finished making the card at $7{:}53\text { p.m.}$
   

**How long did Ella spend making  the birthday card?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] minutes
hints:
To find out how long Ella spent making the birthday card, we can find the distance from $\blueD{7{:}19}$ to $\blueD{7{:}53}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\purpleD1\text{ minute} + \goldE{30}\text{ minutes} + \greenD{3}\text{ minutes}  = 34\text{ minutes}$
Ella spent $34$ minutes making the birthday card.

assessment_items.question_content:
A scuba diver started her dive at $2{:}42 \text { p.m.}$ and finished diving at $3{:}17  \text { p.m.}$
   

**How long did the dive last?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] minutes
hints:
To find out how long the dive was, we can find the distance from $\blueD{2{:}42}$ to $\blueD{3{:}17}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueD{2{:}42} +{} &\goldE8\text{ minutes} = 2{:}50\\\\
{2{:}50} +{} &\purpleD{10}\text{ minutes} = 3{:}00\\\\
3{:}00 +{} & \greenD{10}\text{ minutes} = 3{:}10 \\\\
3{:}10 +{}&\redD{7}\text{ minutes} = \blueD{3{:}17}
\end{align}$
$\goldE8\text{ min} + \purpleD{10}\text{ min} + \greenD{10}  \text{ min} +\redD7\text{ min}  = 35\text{ minutes}$
The dive lasted $35$ minutes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Amy started picking blueberries at  $1{:}37 \text { p.m.}$ and finished picking at $2{:}21\text { p.m.}$
   

**How long did Amy spend picking blueberries?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] minutes
hints:
To find out how long Amy spent picking blueberries, we can find the distance from $\blueD{1{:}37}$ to $\blueD{2{:}21}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueD{1{:}37} +{} &\goldE3\text{ minutes} = 1{:}40\\\\
{1{:}40} +{} &\purpleD{20}\text{ minutes} = 2{:}00\\\\
2{:}00 +{} & \greenD{20}\text{ minutes} = 2{:}20 \\\\
2{:}20 +{}&\redD{1}\text{ minute} = \blueD{2{:}21}
\end{align}$
$\goldE3\text{ min} + \purpleD{20}\text{ min} + \greenD{20}  \text{ min} +\redD1\text{ min}  = 44\text{ minutes}$
Amy spent $44$ minutes picking blueberries.

assessment_items.question_content:
An Olympic cross country skier started her race at $4{:}52 \text { p.m.}$ and finished at $5{:}13  \text { p.m.}$
   

**How long did it take the cross country skier to finish her race?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] minutes
hints:
To find out how long it took the country skier to finish her race, we can find the distance from $\blueD{4{:}52}$ to $\blueD{5{:}13}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueD{4{:}52} +{} &\goldE3\text{ minutes} = 4{:}55\\\\
{4{:}55} +{} &\purpleD{5}\text{ minutes} = 5{:}00\\\\
5{:}00 +{} & \greenD{10}\text{ minutes} = 5{:}10 \\\\
5{:}10 +{}&\redD{3}\text{ minutes} = \blueD{5{:}13}
\end{align}$
$\goldE3\text{ min} + \purpleD{5}\text{ min} + \greenD{10}  \text{ min} +\redD3\text{ min}  = 21\text{ minutes}$
The cross country skier finished her race in $21$ minutes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Daniel started writing a story at $8{:}00 \text { a.m.}$ and finished writing at $11{:}00 \text { a.m.}$
   

**How long did Daniel write?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] hours
hints:
To find out how long it took Daniel to write his story, we can find the distance from $\blueD{8{:}00}$ to $\blueD{11{:}00}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueD{8{:}00} +{} &\goldE1\text{ hour} = 9{:}00\\\\
{9{:}00} +{} &\purpleD{1}\text{ hour} = 10{:}00\\\\
10{:}00 +{} & \greenD{1}\text{ hour} = 11{:}00 
\end{align}$
$\goldE1\text{ hour} + \purpleD{1}\text{ hour} + \greenD{1}  \text{ hour} = 3\text{ hours}$
Daniel wrote for $3$ hours.

assessment_items.question_content:
Frankie started cleaning her house at $1{:}30 \text { p.m.}$ and finished cleaning at $6{:}30  \text { p.m.}$
   

**How long did Frankie clean?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] hours
hints:
To find out how long it took Frankie to clean her house, we can find the distance from $\blueD{1{:}30}$ to $\blueD{6{:}30}$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\goldE1\text{ hour} + \purpleD{1}\text{ hour} + \greenD{1}  \text{ hour} +\maroonD{1}\text{ hour} + \redD{1}\text{ hour} = 5\text{ hours}$
Frankie cleaned for $5$ hours.

assessment_items.question_content:
Adeline started reading a book at $5{:}45 \text { p.m.}$ and finished at $7{:}45  \text { p.m.}$
   

**How long did Adeline spend reading her book?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] hours
hints:
To find out how long it took Adeline to read her book, we can find the distance from $\blueD{5{:}45}$ to $\blueD{7{:}45}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueD{5{:}45} +{} &\goldE1\text{ hour} =6{:}45\\\\
{6{:}45} +{} &\purpleD{1}\text{ hour} = 7{:}45
\end{align}$
$\goldE1\text{ hour} + \purpleD{1}\text{ hour}  =2 \text{ hours}$
Adeline spent $2$ hours reading her book.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jeremy spends $44$ minutes eating dinner with his family.  Jeremy finishes eating dinner at $6{:}09 \text { p.m.}$

**What time did the clock show when Jeremy started eating dinner?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $6{:}09$ when Jeremy finishes dinner.


[[☃ image 1]]

To determine what time Jeremy starts eating dinner we go **left** ${44}$ minutes from $6{:}09$.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
{6{:}09} -{} &\goldE{9}\text{ minutes} = 6{:}00\\\\
{6{:}00} -{} &\purpleD{30}\text{ minutes} = 5{:}30\\\\
5{:}30 -{} & \blueD{5}\text{ minutes} = \greenD{5{:}25} 
\end{align}$

Jeremy starts dinner at $\greenD{5{:}25} $.  

Which clock shows $\greenD{5{:}25}$?

When Jeremy starts eating dinner, the clock shows:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 5 and 6 and the long hand on the last tick mark before the 9.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3f8c822d1deac7f2993a0fbbce9276cb3be79c4f)

![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 6 and 7 and the long hand on the third tick mark after the 10.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/95e3da44f07cb560cd7b5731f2a274cbe430a14d)

![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 5 and 6 and the long hand on the 5.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a53d5dd5427d422fe1d519c9790dfde691f105fa)

assessment_items.question_content:
Andrew spends $32$ minutes doing his homework. He finishes his homework at $4{:}18 \text { p.m.}$

**What time did the clock show when Andrew started his homework?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $4{:}18$ when Andrew finishes his homework.

[[☃ image 1]]

To determine what time Andrew starts his homework we go **left** ${32}$ minutes from $4{:}18$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
{4{:}18} -{} &\goldE{3}\text{ minutes} = 4{:}15\\\\
{4{:}15} -{} &\purpleD{15}\text{ minutes} = 4{:}00\\\\
4{:}00 -{} & \blueD{10}\text{ minutes} = {3{:}50} \\\\
3{:}50 -{} & \maroonD{4}\text{ minutes} = \greenD{3{:}46} \\\\
\end{align}$

Andrew starts his homework at $\greenD{3{:}46} $.  

Which clock shows $\greenD{3{:}46}$?

When Andrew starts his homework, the clock shows:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand on the 5 and the long hand on the 10.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4b15b59ab8274bbba0c60d5f6bdba3cf4e5fd060)

![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 3 and 4 and the long hand on the second tick mark after the 6.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/00566e1eddaeecaf3e207fbb24858ad4533c126a)

![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 3 and 4 and the long hand on the first tick mark after the 9.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a663efedf8d3327238eef1a73fab21d8682834d4)

assessment_items.question_content:
Oliver and Rani play a game of table tennis for $48$ minutes. They finished playing at $9{:}05 \text{ a.m.}$

**What time did the clock show when Oliver and Rani started playing table tennis?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $9{:}05$ when Oliver and Rani finish their game of table tennis.


[[☃ image 1]]

To determine what time Oliver and Rani started their game of table tennis, we go **left** ${48}$ minutes from $9{:}05$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
{9{:}05} -{} &\goldE{5}\text{ minutes} = 9{:}00\\\\
{9{:}00} -{} &\purpleD{30}\text{ minutes} = 8{:}30\\\\
8{:}30 -{} & \blueD{10}\text{ minutes} = {8{:}20} \\\\
8{:}20 -{} & \maroonD{3}\text{ minutes} = \greenD{8{:}17} \\\\
\end{align}$

Oliver and Rani started their game at $\greenD{8{:}17} $.  

Which clock shows $\greenD{8{:}17}$?

When Oliver and Rani started playing table tennis, the clock shows:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 8 and 9 and the long hand on the third tick mark after the 4.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/07aed562bad636b9cf2096065f90fd57a0484601)

assessment_items.question_content:
Zayed builds a model airplane in $24$ minutes.  Zayed finished building at $11{:}31 \text { a.m.}$

**What time did the clock show when Zayed starts building the model airplane?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $11{:}31$ when Zayed finished building his model airplane.

[[☃ image 1]]

To determine what time Zayed started building his model airplane, we go **left** ${24}$ minutes from $11{:}31$.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
{11{:}31} -{} &\goldE{1}\text{ minute} = 11{:}30\\\\
{11{:}30} -{} &\purpleD{20}\text{ minutes} = 11{:}10\\\\
11{:}10 -{} & \blueD{3}\text{ minutes} = \greenD{11{:}07} 
\end{align}$

Zayed started building his model airplane at $\greenD{11{:}07} $.  

Which clock shows $\greenD{11{:}07}$?

When Zayed started building his model airplane, the clock shows:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 11 and 12 and the long hand on the fourth tick mark after the 4.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ed301afb1e93f7bf08dccb12146d82dbcf3b29d9)

assessment_items.question_content:
Saki spends $13$ minutes washing her dog.  Saki finishes washing her dog at $2{:}22 \text{ p.m.}$

**What time did the clock show when Saki starts washing her dog?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $2{:}22$ when Saki finished washing her dog.

[[☃ image 1]]

To determine what time Saki started washing her dog, we go **left** ${13}$ minutes from $2{:}22$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
{2{:}22} -{} &\goldE{2}\text{ minutes} = 2{:}20\\\\
{2{:}20} -{} &\purpleD{10}\text{ minutes} = 2{:}10\\\\
2{:}10 -{} & \blueD{1}\text{ minute} = \greenD{2{:}09} 
\end{align}$

Saki started washing her dog $\greenD{2{:}09} $.  

Which clock shows $\greenD{2{:}09}$?

When Saki starts washing her dog, the clock shows:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 2 and 3 and the long hand on the second tick mark after 4.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b0934a63122d406c79d26b93fb62373f7c388b68)

assessment_items.question_content:
It takes Ernesto $27$ minutes to walk from his house to the science museum. He gets to the museum at $9{:}39 \text { a.m.}$

**What time did the clock show when Ernesto left his house?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $9{:}39$ when Ernesto gets to the museum.

[[☃ image 1]]

To determine what time Ernesto left his house, we go **left** ${27}$ minutes from $9{:}39$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
{9{:}39} -{} &\goldE{9}\text{ minutes} = 9{:}30\\\\
{9{:}30} -{} &\purpleD{10}\text{ minutes} = 9{:}20\\\\
9{:}20 -{} & \blueD{8}\text{ minutes} = \greenD{9{:}12} 
\end{align}$

Ernesto started walking to the museum at $\greenD{9{:}12} $.  

Which clock shows $\greenD{9{:}12}$?

When Ernesto left his house, the clock shows:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 9 and 10 and the long hand on the second tick mark after the 5.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4f423b376f7202da89863764c3d6ba2ef665116f)

![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 9 and 10 and the long hand on the second tick mark after the 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/29428b4644dc0b5887e5ce2e569d86867124b361)

![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 10 and 11 and the long hand on the first tick mark after the 3.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0c4f59b6e3a0e4d0997c84d0dc05d2fdc6a13c3f)

assessment_items.question_content:
Karim spends $52$ minutes playing cards with his grandfather.  Karim starts playing cards at $4{:}06 \text { p.m.}$

**What time does the clock show when Karim finishes playing cards?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $4{:}06$ when Karim starts playing cards.

[[☃ image 1]]

To determine what time Karim finished playing cards, we go **right** ${52}$ minutes from $4{:}06$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
{4{:}06} +{} &\goldE{4}\text{ minutes} = 4{:}10\\\\
{4{:}10} +{} &\purpleD{20}\text{ minutes} = 4{:}30\\\\
4{:}30 +{} & \greenD{20}\text{ minutes} = {4{:}50} \\\\
4{:}50 +{} &\redD{8}\text{ minutes} = \blueD{4{:}58}
\end{align}$

Karim finished playing cards at $\blueD{4{:}58} $.  

Which clock shows $\blueD{4{:}58}$?

When Karim finishes playing cards, the clock shows:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 4 and 5 and the long hand on the first tick mark after the 9.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3535aaeed0017e9f0b7e39aae2e7e2387cddd5aa)

![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand on the 5 and the long hand on the third tick mark after the 11.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fde5f380a58e6d00c8d74bc36513f2197c8983cb)

![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand on the 6 and the long hand on the third tick mark after the 11.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/04aece11c3a3cafc361426eba916b8ae6f730782)

assessment_items.question_content:
Abby spends $37$ minutes making sidewalk chalk drawings.  Abby starts drawing at $2{:}05 \text { p.m.}$

**What  time does the clock show when Abby finishes drawing?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $2{:}05$ when Abby starts drawing.

[[☃ image 1]]

To determine what time Abby finishes drawing, we go **right** ${37}$ minutes from $2{:}05$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
{2{:}05} +{} &\goldE{5}\text{ minutes} = 2{:}10\\\\
{2{:}10} +{} &\purpleD{20}\text{ minutes} = 2{:}30\\\\
2{:}30 +{} & \greenD{10}\text{ minutes} = {2{:}40} \\\\
2{:}40 +{} &\redD{2}\text{ minutes} = \blueD{2{:}42}
\end{align}$

Abby finished drawing at $\blueD{2{:}42} $.  

Which clock shows $\blueD{2{:}42}$?

When Abby finishes drawing, the clock shows:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 2 and 3 and the long hand on the second tick mark after the 6.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3d441736f3fd3b2e15752dba77dd239115aacb19)

assessment_items.question_content:
Antoine's judo practice is $37$ minutes long.  Antoine starts judo practice at $6{:}35 \text { p.m.}$

**What time does the clock show when Antoine finishes judo practice?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $6{:}35$ when Antoine starts judo practice.

[[☃ image 1]]

To determine what time Antoine finishes judo practice, we go **right** ${37}$ minutes from $6{:}35$.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
{6{:}35} +{} &\goldE{10}\text{ minutes} = 6{:}45\\\\
{6{:}45} +{} &\purpleD{15}\text{ minutes} = 7{:}00\\\\
7{:}00 +{} & \greenD{10}\text{ minutes} = {7{:}10} \\\\
7{:}10 +{} &\redD{2}\text{ minutes} = \blueD{7{:}12}
\end{align}$

Antoine finished judo at $\blueD{7{:}12} $.  

Which clock shows $\blueD{7{:}12}$?

When Antoine finishes judo practice, the clock shows:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand on the 6 and the long hand on the third tick mark after the 11.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fe697ce68f5c9be79ce10c2226ec348bf7c68188)

![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand on the 7 and the long hand on the second tick mark after the 1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fe12f82a65b1416f36e81639a497f0a580a52590)

assessment_items.question_content:
It takes Yasemin $50$ minutes to complete her history project.  Yasemin starts her project at $3{:}42 \text { p.m.}$

**What time does the clock show when Yasemin finishes her project?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $3{:}42$ when Yasemin starts her project.

[[☃ image 1]]

To determine what time Yasemin finishes her project, we go **right** ${50}$ minutes from $3{:}42$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
{3{:}42} +{} &\goldE{3}\text{ minutes} = 3{:}45\\\\
{3{:}45} +{} &\purpleD{15}\text{ minutes} = 4{:}00\\\\
4{:}00 +{} & \greenD{30}\text{ minutes} = {4{:}30} \\\\
4{:}30 +{} &\redD{2}\text{ minutes} = \blueD{4{:}32}
\end{align}$

Yasemin finished her project at $\blueD{4{:}32} $.  

Which clock shows $\blueD{4{:}32}$?

When Yasemin finishes her project, the clock shows:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand on the 4 and the long hand on the 10.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/89f4d8ddce50acaa9b047044f95c416cdb802cd1)

![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 4 and 5 and the long hand on the second tick mark after 6.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/aee4f24d0e8329d220de3fe062d3b512c5fd2052)

![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 4 and 5 and the long hand on the second tick mark after 4.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b7165738d12bfa167614b3ed5c4069f41a8a6472)

assessment_items.question_content:
It takes Kaleigh $28$ minutes to bake a cake. She put the cake in the oven at $11{:}54 \text { a.m.}$

**What time does the clock show when Kaleigh finishes baking her cake?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We start at $11{:}54$ when Kaleigh starts baking her cake.

[[☃ image 1]]

To determine what time Kaleigh finishes baking her cake, we go **right** ${28}$ minutes from $11{:}54$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
{11{:}54} +{} &\goldE{6}\text{ minutes} = 12{:}00\\\\
{12{:}00} +{} &\purpleD{20}\text{ minutes} = 12{:}20\\\\
12{:}20 +{} & \greenD{2}\text{ minutes} = \blueD{12{:}22}
\end{align}$

Kaleigh finished baking her cake at $\blueD{12{:}22} $.  

Which clock shows $\blueD{12{:}22}$?

When Kaleigh finishes baking her cake, the clock shows:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 11 and 12 and the long hand on the first tick mark after the 5.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d983fcefc3c07b51f0e50acccfa1084b9f3d15d9)

![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 12 and 1 and the long hand on the second tick mark after the 4.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0b71a8796f5073355eecf080af560b88bdbcfc43)

![A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 12 and 1 and the long hand on the third tick mark after the 5.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/db5a0d9b1e60a9a35a4bb79382d6e38ec5539e7b)
